records.ety: [OE. trechere, trichere, OF. trecherie, tricherie, F. tricherie trickery, from tricher to cheat, to trick, OF. trichier, trechier; probably of Teutonic origin. See Trickery, Trick.]

defs: Violation of allegiance or of faith and confidence; treasonable or perfidious conduct; perfidy; treason.
quotes: Be ware, ye lords, of their treachery.
In the council chamber at Edinburgh, he had contracted a deep taint of treachery and corruption.
